# fly_machine (Resource)

Manages a Fly.io machine. Import using app_name/machine_id: `terraform import fly_machine.example my-app/machine-id`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "fly_app" "example" {
  name     = "my-machine-app"
  org_slug = "personal"
}

resource "fly_machine" "example" {
  app    = fly_app.example.name
  region = "iad"
  image  = "registry.fly.io/my-app:latest"

  guest {
    cpu_kind  = "shared"
    cpus      = 1
    memory_mb = 256
  }

  service {
    protocol      = "tcp"
    internal_port = 8080

    port {
      port     = 80
      handlers = ["http"]
    }

    port {
      port     = 443
      handlers = ["tls", "http"]
    }
  }

  env = {
    PORT = "8080"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `app` (String) The name of the Fly.io application this machine belongs to. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `image` (String) The Docker image to run on the machine.
- `region` (String) The region to deploy the machine in (e.g., 'ord', 'iad'). Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `auto_destroy` (Boolean) Whether to automatically destroy the machine when it exits.
- `check` (Block List) Health check configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--check))
- `cmd` (List of String) The command to run on the machine.
- `cordoned` (Boolean) Whether the machine is cordoned (will not accept new connections).
- `desired_status` (String) The desired status of the machine: 'started', 'stopped', or 'suspended'.
- `entrypoint` (List of String) The entrypoint for the machine.
- `env` (Map of String) Environment variables to set on the machine.
- `guest` (Block, Optional) Guest VM resource configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--guest))
- `metadata` (Map of String) Metadata key-value pairs for the machine.
- `metrics` (Block, Optional) Metrics endpoint configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--metrics))
- `mount` (Block, Optional) Volume mount configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--mount))
- `name` (String) The name of the machine. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `restart` (Block, Optional) Restart policy configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--restart))
- `schedule` (String) Cron-like schedule for the machine.
- `service` (Block List) Services exposed by the machine. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--service))
- `skip_launch` (Boolean) If true, the machine will be created but not started.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the machine was created.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the machine.
- `instance_id` (String) The instance ID of the machine.
- `private_ip` (String) The private IPv6 address of the machine.
- `state` (String) The current state of the machine.
- `updated_at` (String) Timestamp when the machine was last updated.

<a id="nestedblock--check"></a>
### Nested Schema for `check`

Required:

- `name` (String) The name of the health check.
- `type` (String) The type of health check (e.g., 'tcp', 'http').

Optional:

- `interval` (String) How often to run the check (e.g., '10s', '1m').
- `method` (String) The HTTP method for HTTP checks.
- `path` (String) The HTTP path for HTTP checks.
- `port` (Number) The port to check.
- `timeout` (String) Timeout for the check (e.g., '2s').


<a id="nestedblock--guest"></a>
### Nested Schema for `guest`

Optional:

- `cpu_kind` (String) The kind of CPU to use (e.g., 'shared', 'performance').
- `cpus` (Number) Number of vCPUs.
- `gpu_kind` (String) The kind of GPU to attach.
- `gpus` (Number) Number of GPUs.
- `memory_mb` (Number) Memory in megabytes.


<a id="nestedblock--metrics"></a>
### Nested Schema for `metrics`

Optional:

- `path` (String) The path to expose metrics at.
- `port` (Number) The port to expose metrics on.


<a id="nestedblock--mount"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mount`

Optional:

- `add_size_gb` (Number) The size in GB to add when extending the volume.
- `extend_threshold_percent` (Number) The threshold percentage at which to extend the volume.
- `path` (String) The path inside the machine to mount the volume.
- `size_gb_limit` (Number) The maximum size in GB the volume can be extended to.
- `volume` (String) The volume ID to mount. Changing this forces a new resource.


<a id="nestedblock--restart"></a>
### Nested Schema for `restart`

Optional:

- `max_retries` (Number) Maximum number of restart retries.
- `policy` (String) The restart policy (e.g., 'always', 'on-failure', 'no').


<a id="nestedblock--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service`

Required:

- `internal_port` (Number) The port the service listens on inside the machine.
- `protocol` (String) The protocol for the service (e.g., 'tcp', 'udp').

Optional:

- `autostart` (Boolean) Whether the machine should be started automatically when a request is received.
- `autostop` (Boolean) Whether the machine should be stopped automatically when idle.
- `concurrency` (Block, Optional) Concurrency limits for the service. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--service--concurrency))
- `force_https` (Boolean) Whether to force HTTPS on the service.
- `min_machines_running` (Number) Minimum number of machines to keep running.
- `port` (Block List) Port mappings for the service. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--service--port))

<a id="nestedblock--service--concurrency"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service.concurrency`

Optional:

- `hard_limit` (Number) The hard concurrency limit.
- `soft_limit` (Number) The soft concurrency limit.
- `type` (String) The concurrency type (e.g., 'connections', 'requests').


<a id="nestedblock--service--port"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service.port`

Optional:

- `force_https` (Boolean) Whether to force HTTPS for this port.
- `handlers` (List of String) Protocol handlers (e.g., 'http', 'tls').
- `port` (Number) The external port number.
